channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: The US national debt is now bigger than the entire US economy Today's debt milestone is not borne from war mobilization. — 89 replies — 222.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
The US national debt is now bigger than the entire US economy Today's debt milestone is not borne from war mobilization.

Open thread • Replies: 89 • Updated: 2026-05-07 14:06 UTC

OP

The US national debt is now bigger than the entire US economy
Today's debt milestone is not borne from war mobilization. It is the accumulated result of tax cuts without matching reductions in spending, an aging population drawing more heavily on Social Security and Medicare, and years of political leadership on both sides of the aisle that has consistently chosen short-term popularity over long-term solvency.
You fags are cooked (including me)

Early replies (first 3)

62179629
Doesn’t matter, because no one can collect from the US Govt

Refute this without sounding mad

62179630
>>62179622
breaking news: voters want stuff and dont want to pay for it

62179853
>>62179622
AI will fix this

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62184336
>>62179622
>Cut defense spending
>Cut medicare
>Cut social security
>Tax rich people, tax the middle class, tax the working class, tax the poor, tax your mom. Tax your Grandma. Everyone's getting picked up, flipped over around shaken for money and needs to get over it.
>Inflate the currency
>Keep every other program because those those don't actually matter that much. In fact, add more.
>Ignore culture wars. Ignore culture clowns.
>Pay our doomer, meme-brained, low IQ, lazy and angertainment-addicted public a few pennies to go to the park to end the loneliness epidemic and polarization and just go outside and see how human beings function
>Cut the "muh affordability" meme subsidizes. We're adults. Show them the real bill and just stop it.
>Nuclear, Wind and Solar power. Shut up about the rest.
>Viciously murder zoning, car centricity and nimby laws on public television
Amendment guaranteeing that you have to let people build houses on land they buy provided it's not a safety or environmental hazard.
>Respect law outcomes, but persecute each and every corrupt official no matter what to where you make Watergate look like a peace rally compared it.
>Expose every grifter. They might not be prosecutable, but they will be known.
>Spew TRVTHS daily and nightly no matter who it hurts (including yourself)
There. Fixed your country.

Do you think the average American won't do this because they're afraid won't work or because we are afraid of seeing the infeasibility and sunk cost of the current American lifestyle?

62184343
>>62184207
Such as?

62184400
>>62179629
Eventually the dollar collapses though and bond yields spike because people demand higher and higher interest on what's a collapsing dollar

62187354
>>62184058

https://i.4cdn.org/biz/1777750871226027.jpg
https://cdn.donmai.us/sample/a2/1a/__gunsmith_bitch_panty_stocking_with_garterbelt_drawn_by_sketchinventor__sample-a21abc6dafbdafd9ed6c0893528de850.jpg

62188579
>>62181550
This
>>62179928
>Come and take the debt
You are genuinely retarded. Slavery needs to come back, all races.

62188593
>>62179622
>You fags are cooked (including me)

62188936
>>62184343
Surplus: Norway, Denmark
Minor deficit: Sweden

62192220
>>62188593
Yummy

62193530
>>62179629
Food
>America is food secure
Unti AI agriculture was the most subsidized industry in the country.

62193681
>>62184336
Just re-arrange american healthcare and social security system. Private healthcare and private education are incredibly costly.

62193707
>>62184336
The system more than likely is incapable of being fixed at this point and cutting benefits is political suicide.

62193761
>>62193707
The fix is inflation. They'll keep running it hot to inflate away the debt while forcing bond rates lower artificially.

62194225
>>62179622
Doesn't matter its all fabricated when the fed can print infinitely

62194394
>>62179629
The world stopping to use of the US dollar as the de-facto reserve currency and universal trade tender is not something the US in its current state can survive tho.

Any other country in the world would be facing the greatest hyperinflation event ever seen if they did the shit the US is doing. They're the only things barely preventing it right now.

62194405
>>62184336
you assume things aren't being played out exactly as intended

62197937
>>62179622
me when bi dl trade verbal dom

62200684
>>62179622
>The US national debt is now bigger than the entire US economy

is that bad? Most people have more debt than cash in the bank, not a big deal! Plus, Trump loves debt. Its all part of the plan.

62200692
>>62179928
i'm with this guy. eat shit commies

62200774
>>62200684
The sovereign bond ponzi scheme rolling over depends on the active population out numbering the inactive population that demands monthly checks for doing nothing. The inactive population is outnumbering the active population 20:1. No that's not sustainable anymore. The risk the debt carries is irrational the debt is uncovered, it would need to be defaulted on , respective bought up by a bad bank. The former is the dead of the global ponziconomy the latter is asking for the active population defecting and making the debt even more uncovered

The sovereign bond ponzis are over

62200945
>>62179630
Yeah I don't think anyone voted for bank bailouts, PPP loans, corporate tax breaks or the 1.5 trillion dollar military budget

62200948
>>62200684
>people are the same as national economies
RETARD ALARM

62200952
>>62179928
You dumb fuck the debt isn't something that you take from no country whatsoever the point is nobody is lending you any money and then you uscuck are finished

62200965
>>62180253
nigger if we stop paying our debt the entire world's economy collapses and china becomes the new superpower overnight. going to bomb some shithole is not changing any of that

62200972
>>62180253
yeah sure get usabugs nuked for good retarded fuck

62200980
>>62200945
it's more like the government puts up a facade of employment while taking out 3 trillion every year. just dismantle this unconstitutional ass government

62201141
>>62179622
It’s just time for this country to go and fade away and fall asleep forever, hopefully the death throes of a dying empire will be quick with little collateral dmg

62201178
The amount of debt genuinely doesn’t matter at all because the currency keeps inflating, and the currency keeps inflating because we keep injecting money into the system via govt spending, which gets us further in debt. It’s a cyclical infinite money glitch and nobody will stop it because it’s just the way a system that uses fiat currency works

62201273
>>62201178
If the debt doesn't matter Marx, then why doesn't the gubermint just default on the uncovered ponzi schemes and instead needs to force legislate private actors to buy up the toxic trash bond waste

62201365
>>62201273
because that would destroy the legitimacy of the system and they couldn't keep doing it. why the fuck would they do that?

62201624
>>62179622
They will inflate away the debt while keeping the dollar relevant as long as possible by massive military spending (which accomplishes both goals.) The draft will return while American citizens become increasingly desperate and impoverished and the debt gets inflated into nothing. As foreign demand for dollars collapses they will force 401ks and pension funds to buy them for the greater good, and everyone who doesn't own assets will be wiped out. S&P will hit ATH after ATH while bread becomes $50 a loaf. Finally after WW3 they'll restructure the whole economic system into something else altogether.

item.title: It’s not a bubble — 21 replies — 84.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
It’s not a bubble

Open thread • Replies: 21 • Updated: 2026-05-07 14:23 UTC

OP

It’s just a new paradigm

>As of April 2026, Anthropic's annualized revenue run rate has surpassed $30 billion, driven by enterprise API usage, up from approximately $9 billion in late 2025. While rapidly growing, the company is not currently profitable due to massive capital expenditures on AI infrastructure, including a $200 billion, 5-year commitment to cloud services.

Early replies (first 3)

62198820
>ratio
Advertising account that gets swamped into feeds and ignored and likely blocked the moment any human comes across. Irrelevant. Go advertise your ad account somewhere else

62198822
>promise to buy mag7 slop
>mag7 earnings go up
>mag7 then commit to buy more of your slop
>everybody wins

“Not circular”

62198831
>>62198820

Take your medicine

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62198832
and OPis also confirmed a bot.
4chan is worse than moltbook

62198861
>>62198820
>>62198832
Meds

62198865
Who are you social signaling to bot, yourself?

62198890
>>62198817
This is the AI supercycle. Nasdaq is going to 40k. 10x long now or get left behind.

62198903
>>62198890
>my index scam that mostly went number up due to a demographic group that turned negative cashflow last year is going to keep going up
>please buy my bag
how about no. Pension funds have no other option left anymore to fulfil their contractual obligations than to sell into a non existent bid

62200785
>>62198890
Let's say that AI within the next 5-10 years will bring a massive boost to GDP, not just in a numerical sense. But the AI actually produce or help produce things.
This growth of the economy will not be evenly distributed, at the top of my mind then you will have three categories of companies 1) picks and shovels, think Nvidia, Anthropic etc. Companies whose product is AI to some extend. 2) Those whose product can be made by AI, a double edged sword, but a web page design company could either die or benefit significantly from AI. 3) those that will see little, except from "rising tide lift all boats", McDonalds or Walmart will probably see little benefit from AI.

My point is that Nasdaq is a mixed bags of 1,2 and 3. Think the money is in 1, and the big money is in finding a 2 that survive e.g. video game developer that use live-LLM NPC's.

62200795
>>62200785
Thinking mistake. If the demand facing the increased offer is declining the effect on the voodoo numbers of GDP are muted to negative. Ai is a deflation accelerating technology

62200855
>>62200795
Maybe with time.. I could see a scenario where AI leads to increased competitiveness, that the profit margin shrinks. That the barrier to create a game like GTA-6 will get so low, that a hundred of similar quality games will be released every year, and the video game designers will begin competing on price, because the supply is greater than the demand.

But even if this is the case, i still think that AI will reshuffle our economy to such an extend that real money is to be made. A lot of horse-carriage and analogue cameras manufactures has to die. Seems impossible for the AI-enabled-successors of such companies not to capture a real customer base with real money to spend. Especially in the early stages, where the competitive landscape is still immature.

62200858
I'm applying to every dumbass AI job I can because they're remote and no one else is hiring besides the warehouse, so I hope it's not a bubble.

62200878
>>62200858
ai jobs are actually good to applied to because they ask for 5-10 years of experience but no one has it in this particular sector

just get 2-3 certifications and you are good to go

62201449
>>62198822
It is circular but it has to be because nobody else has that kind of money to throw at a start-up

62201474
>>62201449
so you are aware the stock market and pseudo valuations of the ponzi shares is just a circular re-hypotecization ponzi scheme that is 100% dependent on the collateral not going tits up. Then you understand that ponzi is at the end of its life with the primary underlying collateral, the sovereign ponzi scheme deterministically dying due to demographic realities and the majority cashflow and credit demand that came from an overhang of geriatric demographic that was a net positive cashflow for the past 40 years now having turned into a net negative cashflow

Anybody still holding onto Boomer ponzis in the late 2020s is asking to be fucked in the ass and lose literally everything

62201503
>>62200855
Is "our" economy in the room with you now?
you fucking idiots simply dont get offer and demand like the typical communist scum. The "economy" of the past 60 years on a GLOBAL SCALE was never an economy but a purely on hopes, dreams, overrolled unvovered sovereign bond ponzis and the assumption of an ever growing base of suckers based ponziconomy.

You cant solve demand side issues on the production side no matter how much more efficient you make productivity. If there is a diminishing demand, be it for goods and services or credit demand the ponzi is cooked. The fantasy valuations go line down, the potential technological productivity increase is simply not needed, let alone is able to make it so the valuation of the collateral doesn't go line down as well, collapsing the entire ponzi and burring the boomer paradigms and plans under it

62201529
the worst of all this shit is that I did not buy motherfucker MUU when it dropped to 263 before rocketing to 470 after earnings. That's ONE FUCKING WEEK and it nearly 60%.

62201637
>>62198817
a 100% legit look at op for the last 2 years

62201638
>>62201474
>so you are aware the stock market and pseudo valuations of the ponzi shares is just a circular re-hypotecization ponzi scheme that is 100% dependent on the collateral not going tits up.
Yes you fucking retard, stock prices depend on the companies not going bankrupt.

62201644
>>62201503
you should get more mad and blog more on 4chan for free.

62201687
>>62201638
wrong, stock valuation depends on a sucker buying out somebody trying to cash out. In the concrete case today that means a pension fund that has a legal obligation to pay out liquidity to a geriatric demographic overhang has somehow to find buyers for that ponzi paper boomer Joe bought for his 401K 40 years ago in a market with less buyers. Offer and demand, if the demand is lower than the offer the price goes down; if that price is already a function of a re-hypotecization pyramid ponzi scheme, independent of the company being bankrupt or not, producing something or not, the stock valuation has a real probability going to literally 0

item.title: I’ve been without work now for a few weeks and this is the saddest state of existance i’ve ever been in. — 14 replies — 56.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
I’ve been without work now for a few weeks and this is the saddest state of existance i’ve ever been in.

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-05-07 14:22 UTC

OP

I’ve been without work now for a few weeks and this is the saddest state of existance i’ve ever been in. I’m just completely alone in my apartment with nothing important to do, lonely. I might as well not exist at all. It’s like i’m the biggest loser in high school or something. The people here who advocate for the NEET lifestyle must be some real freaks.

Early replies (first 3)

62201436
I am truly sorry to hear that you dont have anything meaningful to do in your life or have an identity besides being a wageslave. This is entirely a (you) problem

62201447
Apartment? Let me guess youre living in some shitty city surrounded by brown people in some shitty apartment and youre wondering why your life is shit. You need to fix your environment, you do not need to hypnotize yourself by working some bullshit job that you dont care about

62201460
>>62201428
I'm sorry to say that you've been brainwashed. If you can't do anything but work then you should kill yourself. Actually don't because we need people like you to do "important things" like putting our fries in the bag

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62201464
>>62201428
>workout
>cook healthy food
>clean up place
>work on some project
>research cryptos
>tweet multiple times per day shilling your coins
>go for walks/hike/bike
>go kayaking/canoeing/jet skiing
>unironically have sex
>shit post on the Chans
>play vidya
>watch podcasts and shit
>go to church
>read sacred scripture
>read about the saints

My brother in Christ there is so much to do when you don’t have to wage slave.

62201502
>>62201428
Kek you are retarded. 95% of wagies dont do important stuff.

62201519
>>62201436
upvoted

62201543
>>62201436
It’s called having an important job where you do your part of taking care of the society, not ”wage-slaving”.

62201555
>>62201543
>yay I'm doing my part contributing to a rigged system designed to destroy everything my ancestors held sacred and glorify everything depraved!

62201557
>>62201543
Yeah society is totally worth beeing takes care of... Totally important...

62201578
>>62201428
The only time I'm moving away from my house is to live in a mega city.
You just live poor

62201588
>>62201464
>workout
>cook healthy food
>clean up place
>go for walks/hike/bike
>go kayaking/canoeing/jet skiing

Yeah these are nice but ultimately i still feel fucking lonely, like i’m just selfimproooving without any reason for it. While i could be learning valuable skills at work.

>work on some project
>research cryptos
>tweet multiple times per day shilling your coins

This is just work but with a shitty uncertain pay-off.

>unironically have sex

Women have a lot more sex with you when you work, especially if it’s a high status, high paying job.

>shit post on the Chans
>play vidya
>watch podcasts and shit

Are you unironically 16? How does an adult find this in any way a good way to spend time? Except as a price for hard work.

>go to church
>read sacred scripture
>read about the saints
Yeah religion has some nice philosophy in it but most of it is a waste of time that could be spend in work actually helping people.

All in all i don’t understand how you can just enjoy wasting time without any purpose. It’s nice to take a few months long vacation every now and then if you’re feeling really exhausted, but other than that just no.

62201612
>>62201555
>>62201557
You guys just sound like you were retarded enough to get mindbroken by the internet information flood. Our ancestors would have fought many wars in order to be able to have the living standards we have in our societies.

>>62201578
>>62201447
City or countryside, which is it? I’m from the countryside, it really isn’t much different doing nothing at all in a forest. Walks are nice but you need a purpose besides them too. I have money saved so i can live upper middle class lifestyle for three months before my next work starts. All in all this just feels fucking lonely, like how a very unpopular loser kid would live.

62201672
>>62201612
>We shall fight to the last man to ensure that our children are replaced from their own homeland and their work devalued to the point they cannot even afford shelter, but at least they have glowing rectangles to stare at while everything collapses

62201684
>>62201612
Living standards? Who cares about that shit as long as there is food which is guaranteed by modern technological farming? They would be shocked seeing we get invaded by third worlders while having feminism.

item.title: link can't even get to 10. it can't even fucking get to 10. you people SUCK lmao. just know that we're never gonna let y… — 11 replies — 44.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
link can't even get to 10. it can't even fucking get to 10. you people SUCK lmao. just know that we're never gonna let y…

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-05-07 14:28 UTC

OP

link can't even get to 10. it can't even fucking get to 10. you people SUCK lmao. just know that we're never gonna let you live it down. you had your one big chance and you blew, it stinkers. now everything is so fucking expensive and you're still struggling to achieve prices that were bad 5 years ago. don't you get it stinkbags. YOU LOST THE GAME

Early replies (first 3)

62195921
>>62195555
Check the chart, you lost tranny

62195940
>>62195555
Checked and hello mentally ill faggot how are you doing today?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62196514
>>62195644

62196975
>>62195921
just did. sub 10
you lost, stinker

62200283
bump

62200651
Of all the avatars to assume to go along with the spam, why did the tranny pick a little girl?

62201619
>>62200651
shut up, stinkbag

62201632
$10 is a reasonable price for what chainlink does. People don't like to speculate on chainlink's price because it's not very volatile and most people don't even know what it does.

62201653
i sold at about $35 years ago since it was a 5x for me and i still can't believe people held after it came down from that massive pump

62201703
>>62201653
I took out my initial plus $10k at ~$37 and put the rest of my stack in the pool for rewards
If this shit ever moves again it's literally free money

item.title: It feels like the powers that be are determined to drive bobos extinct — 5 replies — 19.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
It feels like the powers that be are determined to drive bobos extinct

Open thread • Replies: 5 • Updated: 2026-05-07 14:14 UTC

OP

Dumping is no longer allowed. Printing money by US govt and wild stock market valuations, among other things. Do you anons think crypto will be the next thing pumped to keep the madness going or is it just hopium?

Early replies (first 3)

62200750
40,000$ in October 2026

Sell in May and go away

62200752
Bobos are about to see the biggest crash in history, they'll be eating good

62200756
if prices drop its over so thats why

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62200781
>>62200742
>Dumping is no longer allowed.
Tell this to the eth mms pls!

62201660
>>62200742
>being a bobo
>when government cant pay its bills
kekaroo

item.title: Not one thread or comment about CLPR — 9 replies — 9.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Not one thread or comment about CLPR

Open thread • Replies: 9 • Updated: 2026-05-07 13:33 UTC

OP

Hedera quitely dropped a bridgeless cross-ledger protocol (CLPR, pronounce clipper)

So, you could trade between all chains as easy as poof ?

How dead is Chainlink ?

Early replies (first 3)

62200031
>>62200024
checked
this is so unimportant its almost comical

62200056
>>62200024
ohnonono

62200072
only thing more pathetic than linkies is hedera dorks

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62200082
These 2010 bot threads become more ridiculous by the day

62200099
>>62200024
The suppression in this thread is crazy, I think we are onto something. Ain't a bot nigga bitch de la pizza française.

62200105
>state proofs
these require:
validator node software itself to be modified to communicate with the validators of other blockchains, which is both unscalable and a governance nightmare.

or a smart contract implementation with third party intermediaries, which brings us back to square one.

62201361
>>62200105
you sound smart, makes me think you're a CLL chad

62201458
>>62200105
shit man, the guy with a phd from carnegie mellon should’ve just asked you. can we have a look at your protocol?

62201484
>>62201458
>appeal to authority
Absolutely disgusting

item.title: KELPDAO bends the knee and pays up — 18 replies — 9.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
KELPDAO bends the knee and pays up

Open thread • Replies: 18 • Updated: 2026-05-07 12:31 UTC

OP

Ooofff not a good look for so called Chainlink killers (kek)

https://x.com/kelpdao/status/2051755467328913637
>After the recent LayerZero exploit, we are taking steps to ensure rsETH is fully secure, which is why we are migrating to @chainlink CCIP.

>From the April 18 incident, it is clear that LayerZero's own infrastructure was exploited, resulting in $300M in losses across DeFi. Independent reports from SEAL 911, Chainalysis, and other major leading security researchers all point to the same origin.

Early replies (first 3)

62193835
>>62193799
Yikes
It’s become blatantly clear that there’s only one oracle solution

62193937
>>62193799
>it is clear that LayerZero's own infrastructure was exploited
Just because you assert something doesn't make it true

62193998
fuck stinks
hate em all
think they suck
simple as

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62194150
i think it's great that chainlink is giving them access for free in exchange for negative tweets about their competition. link marines gladly pay for this drama

62194249
fuck you fuck you fuck you fuck you fuck you fuck you fuck you fuck you fuck you fuck you fuck you fuck you

62194629
KelpDao's CEO should be made to explain the retarded decision on using the bare minimum security setup in the first place in front of audience. These twitter posts of "here's what happened it's not my fault" don't cut. These people need shaming so other developers can get clued in to stop embarrassing the industry with their lazy shitty security decisions.

62194668
>>62193937
the bridge is secured by a single layerzero validator. so either they were exploited or the validator did the crime. not a good look either way

62194688
chainlink are so euphoric about this that rory has made rare appearances in both telegram AND reddit to trumpet the news

62195824
>>62193937
how can you defend a service that had 1 secure node and no decentralization?

62195845
HAHAHAHAHHAHAHAHAHAH SUCKS TO SUCK
ENJOY THE 300M HACK YOU STUPID FAGGOTS

LAYERZERO (ZERO FUNDS INDEED) GOT ABSOLUTELY MOGGED IN PUBLIC AND CANT EVER RECOVER FROM THIS

SERGEY'S BEEN TOLD YA

>>62193937
>>62194249
>>62194688
Who of you is this? You just got hidden in the replies LOL
https://nitter.poast.org/kupr1k/status/2051763779315073237


No amount of third world screeching (pic related) or Nigerian hand wringing will make KelpDAO (or anyone who has seen this shitshow) go back to the LZ scammers. They are TOAST, no respectable body will EVER risk their shit by working with these sort of lowlife bad actors.

62196041
>>62194629
Doesnt matter considering LZs own infra was literally hacked so kys

62196063
>>62194729

62196734
>>62196041
And they still went for the bare minimum security regardless. Not only did they go with a low tier vendor in terms of security, they went with the bare minimum standard of what the low tier vendor offered. Then they should explain both of those in front of others when confessing their sins to the public of their negligence on security for a $300M protocol.

No conflicts with my overall point.

62196988
>>62196041
L0 also havent addressed how their own system got hacked yet. Its gunna be funny.

62197022
>>62193799
chainlink is the only serious player in all of crypto it's embarrassing

62198135
Do you reckon this dude is lurking about these threads?

https://x.com/the_dude__80/status/2052078826809663819

LMAO THE IMPOTENT GAPED BROWNS

62201323
>>62193835
Still no benefit for token holders

item.title: You kids are gonna experience dotcom — 15 replies — 8.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
You kids are gonna experience dotcom

Open thread • Replies: 15 • Updated: 2026-05-07 12:41 UTC

OP

You can thank the grifter in chief for dumping so much liquidity and bullshit tweets for the algos that we are actually going to get THE blowoff top as CPI is set to print 4.5% next week

Couldn’t be a more fitting ending. I mean, how else could you top bankrupting a casino?

Crassus would blush

Early replies (first 3)

62200123
Dotcom and gfc are going to look like a mild correction compared to 60 years of pyramid ponzi scheming on a deflating demographic popping. The bond market is 2 months away from literally dying

62200131
>>62200106
never happening the govt will keep pumping the markets it will never crash like dotcom ever again.

62200139
>>62200131
Aha, that's why the legislator is already so desperate that it forces private companies to buy into the bankrupt sovereign bond ponzi scheme. Lol

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62200179
>>62200106
I've been shorting the market since biden's first and only term. AMA

62200236
>>62200123
As if debt matters anymore. It’s just accounting; doesn’t matter if it’s positive or negative.

62200242
>>62200123
how can I take advantage of this with barely any funds?

62200244
>>62200179
anime girls y/n?

62200265
>>62200236
What do you think is the collateral for the numbers on a screen that is your bank account and 401k?

62200289
>>62200242
Find boomer stocks with low implied volatility on their options. Look at Honda or Siriusxm. You can buy near otm long dated calls for pretty cheap. If you think they v shape recover then your cheap calls go ballistic.

62200345
>>62200289
>Calls
Fucking asshole

62200621
>>62200106
The official response is new paradigm: Waging consumers do not matter anymore, it's only about rich people getting their circular economy. With Palantir, Google, Cloudflare, Criteo and the likes, and AI thought guardrails, the poorscum can be made die off silently within two generations, to make room for a better world of rich people. Remember, Holodomor happened, and commies stayed in power for more than half a century. This time,

62201300
>>62200244
Kirara is the sluttist GoPri and pleases old men for financial gain

62201322
>>62200621
>duuude famines happened 100 years ago so everyone would be okay with a famine today

62201334

It will continue to make money and be profitable, over evaluated start ups will be destroyed.
Just buy APPL and you will be somewhat shielded from the ai bubble burst

62201342
>>62201334

item.title: You faggots are always moaning there's no more insider info on this board so here's one for you. — 7 replies — 3.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
You faggots are always moaning there's no more insider info on this board so here's one for you.

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-05-07 12:41 UTC

OP

You faggots are always moaning there's no more insider info on this board so here's one for you. Very small company, listed on the Amsterdam stock exchange. So insignificant the news haven't picked up on it yet.

They had a massive database fuckup where saved locations and routes were deleted.
The issue is impacting both individual users and fleet operators. Sounds insignificant, but these are extremely important for truck drivers and logistics, especially because European roads are far less suitable for heavy vehicles than North American ones, so all truckers rely heavily on pre-saved routes and locations rather than inputting addresses to reach their destination without getting stuck in a narrow village street or striking a low bridge.

Check TomTom's social media and you will see how big this is in the sector. TomTom has been completely silent about the blackout so far. They are about to lose a large chunk of their paid users and corporate customers.
There are talks of entire freight routes being put on hold while transport managers scramble to sign up to a different provider.

I don't know how to profit from this but I'm sure some of you spergs will. TomTom is about to go down BIGLY.

And if you want to stretch this into a happening, apparently the blackout was a result of some AWS bug/error but it's still unknown if Amazon themselves will be impacted (probably not, if I could bet I'd bet on it being some dumb pajeet hired by TomTom who doesn't know how to work with AWS.)

You're welcome.

Early replies (first 3)

62200697
>>62200674
im not reading all of that sar

62200711
>>62200674
>Amsterdam stock exchange

62200729
Bullish for Rand McNally :0

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62200767
I'm not going to short a stock based on some anonymous post

62200859
>>62200674
I’m assuming they fell for the AI is ready to take over jobs meme and the AI decided to nuke the server for no reason.

62200876
>>62200674
Nigger I checked their twitter and it’s literally just 3 people complaining. Big my ass.

62201343
>>62200876
Pot bellied truckers don't use troonitter. Check their FB group.
/groups/675362882543804

There's still time. Their main competitor is already up 3%

item.title: Anyone here mining Nerva (XNV) with CPU only? — 3 replies — 2.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Anyone here mining Nerva (XNV) with CPU only?

Open thread • Replies: 3 • Updated: 2026-05-07 13:15 UTC

OP

I’ve been experimenting with CPU mining on a small Linux setup and recently came across Nerva (XNV). What caught my attention is that it’s intentionally designed to stay CPU-friendly and resist GPUs/ASICs, kind of like how Monero used to feel in the early days.

I’m not doing this for big profits, mostly out of curiosity and to put some older hardware to use. So far, the hashrate vs power draw has been surprisingly reasonable on an older Ryzen machine.

Has anyone here compared their results with Monero or other CPU-mineable coins? I’d be interested to hear real-world experiences and whether it’s still worth experimenting with CPU mining in 2026.

Early replies (first 3)

62200158
I grabbed a stack of Nerva along with a couple of other privacy micro caps in case they blow up. Nerva looks especially promising with the amount of new development and community activity.

62201275
>>62200158
>Nerva looks especially promising with the amount of new development and community activity.
Most shill-like sentence I've ever seen.

>>62197039
>designed to stay CPU-friendly and resist GPUs/ASICs, kind of like how Monero used to feel
When did Monero stop being CPU friendly and resisting ASICs and GPUs?

62201422
>>62201275
New core release w/ mining speed improvements
New GUI wallet release
New android support release
Nerva Quest community rewards platform release
Nervault online web wallet released

>shill
Sure lil bud, whatever makes you happy.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

No additional replies in the latest window.

62200395
>>62197194
>I Love this Timeline
I Love this Timeline
>I Love this Timeline
I Love this timeline

62200424
>Two more witnesses showed up in the JPMorgan sex abuse case in the refiled legal suit by a former JPMorgan Chase banker, Chirayu Rana, against Lorna Hajdini on the Manhattan Supreme Court docket on Monday, May 4.
>The latest filing includes the testimony of a family friend who claims to have witnessed the intoxicated behaviour of Hajdini. This incident reportedly took place in a New York City apartment where Rana was invited for a threesome.
>Rana’s friend stated in the document that he was woken up by Hajdini in the middle of the night. The witness also mentioned that a “completely naked” Hajdini woke him while he was sleeping on the couch.
>She lit a cigarette and asked Rana to “join them” in the bedroom. When Rana refused, Hajdini allegedly told him, “You know I own [redacted], so you better come join.”
>The man further claimed that after she returned to the bedroom, he could hear Rana pleading, “No, no, no, you have to leave. I’m not going to do this. Please stop.”
>Thereafter, Hajdini left the apartment, and a stunned Rana emerged. The witness then claimed that Rana shared how Hajdini had involved him in sexual behaviour on multiple occasions.
>Rana also shared how Hajdini allegedly blackmailed him and put him in trouble if he refused to comply.
>Another witness, possibly the owner of the apartment where the family friend was staying, claimed he saw Hajdini kissing Rana’s neck on the street. This incident was reportedly from the summer of 2024.
>The witness also claimed that in September 2024, she overheard them entering her apartment building when Hajdini said something like, “I own you, Brownie.”

62176226
>>62176214
>In many cases, nodes must stake LINK before they can provide and be compensated for their services. If they provide incorrect data, they are penalized and lose a portion of the LINK they have staked. This system helps ensure the integrity of Chainlink’s services while also
reducing the free-floating supply of LINK.
Speaking in present tense. I assume this and the and the other clues we've seen in the past couple weeks mean staking imminent?

62195773
>>62195762

62196215
Pretty sad how the LINK shills cling onto their delusion of financial freedom in the year 2017+9

62196318
>>62177980
Its kinda crazy that the whole amount of link left is only worth 2b while ripple dumps this every month lmao

62196466
>>62196318
They should sell it all at once so we can buy the crash and make it during the ensuing bullrun

62199072
>>62192644
Yeah no one has confirmed it
... except the actual parties themselves saying directly in interviews that they're paying for it

kill yourself, subhuman faggot

62199117
>>62195762
I get what you're doing anon, but the responses just seem pointless. I used to get into these "debates" with these types, but you realize these people either hold massive stacks themselves and have a number of reasons they do this, or they're mentally ill, and there's no point because they'll never stop.

Just look at it from an incentive perspective, lets say they don't hold link and they're just trying to have a debate, OK, why waste more time than needed to tell someone they're wrong? They don't do this in xrp threads, or literally anything else on this board. So at that point they're mentally ill and you're wasting your time.

OK, let's say they do hold link. Well, they're either fudding in the 4chan style of "I hate people liking what I like" and trying to gatekeep, in which case they'll never break because this is what people do here. Or they are angry because they bought high and trying to rationalize with themselves why they haven't sold, in which case nothing you say will ease their pain because only the price going up can make them happy.

Nothing about them is good faith, it screams either trolling, mental illness, or bitter resentment, possibly a combination of all 3 in some cases. And at this point, the people who understand link and know what's being built aren't enthused enough to counter this, so unless you want to commit the same sort of negative mental energy, they'll just overwhelm you with quantity over quality.

62199240
>>62199117
>don't argue with holders
>don't argue with non-holders
>just stay in our ebin echo chamber
>let's just XRPmaxx
Interesting

62199263
>fuuuuck every siiiingle stiiiiink

62199326
>>62199117
having fudders in a thread is usually bullish, and I enjoy quickly replying to them!

62199336
>linkie pisses himself
>dude thats gross
>linkie: i won

62199852
>>62184641
their business model is genius. doesnt benefit holders of the funding token at all, but you cant say it its not a brilliant model for CLL profits

62199891
>>62199852
>doesnt benefit holders of the funding token at all
it does, the funding token, LINK, is staked and used as payment for oracle nodes. someone could try to pay nodes to break the network. Without link, the network doesn't have full economic security.
>>62176214
there are more reasons LINK has value that are not listed here

62199896
>>62199891

62199899
>>62199336
You scared of a little bit of pee? The pharma industry has raped your mind to prevent you from drinking the elixer of the gods.

62200039
>>62199240
Nobody's in an echo chamber fuckhead, its a hurtbox, there hasn't been a positive no fud link thread on this board in at least 5 years. I'm just saying most of the people fudding aren't doing it in good faith, they're just bitching for a variety of reasons related to impatience, trolling, and mental illness. Or they're too cowardly to follow their own ambition, sell, and move on with their life. Nobody's shilling you link anon, and based on your posts in this thread you sound deeply unhappy, my advice to you would be to sell and focus on something else, its clear you either need money right away or simply do not believe there's a long term play here.

62200053
>>62200039
